'1,320p' doc/experiences/PROOF.md\"","aggregated_output":"# Proof Experience\n\n本文件只记录 `coq/generated/<name>_proof_manual.v` 的手工证明经验。\n\n## 1. 证明范围\n\n- 只记录 manual proof\n- 不记录 invariant/assert/symexec\n- 不记录 Coq 编译与路径问题\n- 如果 `proof_manual.v` 里没有需要手工证明的 theorem，就直接跳过 manual proof 和 `proof_reasoning.md`\n- 如果 `proof_manual.v` 或 `goal_check.v` 还没有编译通过，就不能退出 proof 阶段，必须继续证明\n\n## 2. 开始前先读当前目标\n\n- 先读 `goal.v`\n- 再读 `proof_auto.v`\n- 再读 `goal_check.v`\n- 先确认当前 witness 的前置条件、结论和上下文变量\n- 第一轮分析先写入 `logs/proof_reasoning.md`\n\n## 3. 先做最短证明骨架\n\n- 先试 `pre_process`\n- 再试 `entailer!`\n- 纯算术部分优先交给 `lia`\n- 每一轮失败后，都要把新的失败点、原因判断和下一步计划追加写入 `logs/proof_reasoning.md`\n\n## 4. 先分清卡点类型\n\n- 算术问题\n- 改写问题\n- 结构展开问题\n- exist / case split 问题\n- 辅助引理缺失\n\n不要一上来就重写整段证明。\n\n## 5. 编译失败时先看完整 proof state\n\n- 不能只看 `coqc` 的单行报错\n- 应优先用 `coqtop` 进入当前定理，查看具体假设、子目标和 tactic 后状态\n- 首先搞清楚当前真正缺的是什么，再决定改 proof、加 rewrite 还是补引理\n\n## 6. 卡住时去例子库检索\n\n- proof 卡住时，按 `doc/retrieval/INDEX.md` 检索相关例子\n- 优先看 `CAV/examples/`\n- 如果 `CAV/examples/` 没有足够接近的例子，再扩大到整个 `QCP_examples/`\n- 检索后要明确写出：当前目标为什么证不出来，现有例子提供了什么可复用 proof pattern\n\n## 7. 主 witness 不要硬顶 tactic，先抽 helper lemma\n\n如果主 witness 同时承担下面几件事：\n\n- 展开结构\n- 改写列表/数组表达式\n- 处理算术 side condition\n- 证明核心语义结论\n\n就不要继续在主 witness 里硬顶 tactic。\n\n更稳的做法是：\n\n- 主 witness 只负责 `Exists`、`Intros`、`subst`、`rewrite`、调用引理\n- 把反复出现的 list / arithmetic / sublist / append / max-min 事实先抽成 helper lemma\n- helper lemma 直接写在当前 `coq/generated/<name>_proof_manual.v`\n\n主 witness 越短，越容易编译稳定；复杂 proof 应该沉到辅助引理里。\n\n## 8. `Cannot find witness` 往往不是神秘错误，而是 side condition 不显式\n\n这类报错常见于：\n\n- `lia` 需要非空条件，但上下文里没显式写出来\n- 需要长度事实，但只有隐含的 `Zlength` 关系\n- `Znth` / `sublist` / `replace_Znth` 的边界条件还没固定\n\n遇到这类错误时，先不要继续换 tactic。\n\n优先补这些显式事实：\n\n- 非空\n- 长度\n- 边界\n- 索引范围\n- 当前分支下的等式关系\n\n经验上，很多 `Cannot find witness` 在补完这类中间 `assert` 后就会消失。\n\n## 9. Coq 脚本尽量写保守，不要过度依赖自动化和自动命名\n\n更稳的写法是：\n\n- 少用复杂的 intro-pattern\n- 少依赖自动命名的 `IH`\n- 中间事实用 `assert` 明确命名\n- 重写后把关键索引和等式显式化简\n\n不稳的写法通常是：\n\n- 一步塞太多 pattern\n- 指望 `lia` 自动猜出所有边界\n- 依赖当前 Coq 版本恰好给出的 `IH` 名字或简化顺序\n\n保守脚本虽然稍长，但在不同 Coq 版本和不同 proof state 下更稳定。\n\n## 10. `entailer!` 收不掉时先整理上下文\n\n- 补 `Intros`\n- 补 `subst`\n- 补 `rewrite`\n- 分开处理 separation logic 部分和纯命题部分\n\n## 11. 失败记录必须写首个稳定错误\n\n- 记录文件\n- 记录行号\n- 记录原始错误文本\n- 记录为什么这个错误会出现\n\n## 12. 不允许绕过证明\n\n- 不允许 `Admitted.`\n- 不允许新增 `Axiom`\n\n## 13. 改结构后必须重新 symbolic\n\n- 改了注释\n- 改了题目专用 `.v`\n- 改了证明结构\n\n都必须重新对齐 witness。\n\n## 14. `proof_auto.v` 已经定义的 lemma，不要在 `proof_manual.v` 里重复定义\n\n- 开始写 manual proof 前，先看一眼 `proof_auto.v`\n- 如果某个 `proof_of_<name>_*` 已经在 `proof_auto.v` 里给出，就不要在 `proof_manual.v` 里再写同名 lemma\n- 否则 `goal_check.v` 里同时 `Include proof_auto` 和 `Include proof_manual` 时会报重复 label\n\n## 15. max/min 扫描题的 helper lemma 值得模板化\n\n对 max/min 扫描类题目，反复会用到这些 lemma：\n\n- 前缀中任意元素都不超过当前 prefix max\n- 如果所有元素都不超过某个上界，则 prefix max 也不超过这个上界\n- 在列表尾部追加一个元素后，max/min 的单调性\n- 追加元素不改变当前 max/min 的条件\n\n这类题的主 witness 通常不难，难点在这些纯 list helper lemma。\n\n所以更稳的策略是：\n\n- 尽早把它们沉淀成局部模板\n- 主 witness 只做“把循环状态翻译成 prefix 语义，再调用 lemma”\n\n不要每道 max/min 扫描题都从零在主 witness 里手搓同一套推理。\n\n## 16. 如果 return witness 同时出现 `x` 和 `x_pre`，先检查 annotation 是否保留了 `x == x@pre`\n\n典型现象：\n\n- `return_wit` 看起来只差把当前参数名改回 `*_pre`\n- proof 里缺的不是 list / arithmetic，而是“这个标量参数从头到尾没变”\n\n处理顺序：\n\n1. 先看 loop invariant 和 loop-exit assertion 是否显式保留了 `x == x@pre`\n2. 如果没有，回 annotation 层补这个不变关系\n3. 清理 generated 文件并重新跑 `symexec`\n\n不要在 `proof_manual.v` 里硬证一个 annotation 本该直接保留的参数恒等关系。\n\n## 17. 字符串/数组复制题如果只知道“当前位置读到 0”，先检查 contract 是否排除了中间 0\n\n典型现象：\n\n- `return_wit` 最后只剩：\n  - `replace_Znth i 0 (l1 ++ d1) = l ++ [0]`\n  - 或等价的 `CharArray.full ...` 目标\n- 已知条件只有：\n  - 前缀 `l1` 与 `l` 在 `< i` 上相等\n  - `Znth i (l ++ [0]) = 0`\n  - 长度关系\n\n这时不要默认继续堆 tactic。\n\n先判断当前 contract 是否真的能推出：\n\n- `i = n`\n- 或者 `l` 在 `0 <= k < n` 上都非零\n\n如果都没有，那么 return witness 往往不是 proof 技巧问题，而是 specification 本身没有排除中间 `0`，导致“在第一个 `0` 处停止复制”不足以推出“整个目标数组等于 `l ++ [0]`”。\n\n处理顺序：\n\n1. 先用 `coqtop` 看清剩余目标是否已经退化成纯 list equality / `CharArray.full` equality\n2. 如果是，就检查 precondition / ghost predicate 是否提供“唯一终止符”或“前缀元素非零”信息\n3. 如果没有，优先记录为 contract gap，不要在 manual proof 里盲目硬证\n\n## 18. 字符串扫描类 return witness 常见的真正桥接点是先证 `i = n`\n\n对 `string_copy`、`strlen` 这类题，`return_wit` 最后看起来常常像：\n\n- `replace_Znth i 0 (...) = l ++ [0]`\n- 或等价的 `CharArray.full ... |-- CharArray.full ...`\n\n这时不要直接盯着最后一条 `replace_Znth`。\n\n更稳的顺序是：\n\n1. 先看当前假设能不能推出 `i = n`\n2. 再用前缀相等性质证明 `l1 = l`\n3. 最后再去化简 `replace_Znth` / `replace_nth`\n\n如果一开始不先拿到 `i = n`，后面的尾部归一化通常会越写越乱。\n\n## 19. `Cannot find witness` 经常意味着长度信息还没展开到 `lia` 能直接用的形状\n\n常见卡点：\n\n- `Zlength (x :: l) = ...`\n- `Zlength nil = ...`\n- “尾部长度等于 1，所以列表只能是 `x :: nil`”\n\n这类地方只写 `lia` 往往不够。\n\n更稳的做法是先显式展开：\n\n- `Zlength_cons`\n- `Zlength_nil`\n- `Zlength_nonneg`\n\n必要时先单独证明一个局部事实，例如：\n\n- 某个尾表长度恰好为 `1`\n- 因此它一定是 `x :: nil`\n\n把这层桥接写出来后，`lia` 才会稳定。\n\n## 20. `replace_Znth` 最后一步经常卡在 `nat` 索引没有化简\n\n即使在 `Z` 上已经知道索引等于 `0`，展开\n\n- `replace_Znth`\n\n之后，Coq 里常常还残着：\n\n- `replace_nth (Z.to_nat (...)) ...`\n\n如果这个 `Z.to_nat (...)` 不显式改成 `0%nat`，`simpl` 很可能不会继续。\n\n稳定写法是：\n\n1. `unfold replace_Znth`\n2. 显式把 `Z.to_nat (...)` 改成 `0%nat`\n3. 再 `simpl`\n\n很多看起来像“最后一条列表相等还差一点”的目标，真正缺的就是这一步。\n\n## 21. Coq 脚本优先用保守写法，尤其少依赖脆弱的 `destruct ... as ...` 形状\n\n自动生成或半自动修改的 proof 中，以下写法更容易在不同环境里出问题：\n\n- 复杂 `intro-pattern`\n- 嵌套很深的 bullet\n- `destruct ... as ...` 紧跟多个分支和局部重写\n\n更稳的替代是：\n\n- 先 `destruct`\n- 再在分支里单独 `rewrite`\n- 需要结构信息时，先证明一个局部 `assert`\n\n这样脚本会更长，但通常更稳，也更容易在下一轮编译失败时精确定位 first blocker。\n","exit_code":0,"status":"completed"}}

I’m writing the first proof log entry and then patching `proof_manual.v` with those helper lemmas plus a first full proof attempt.

Lemma replace_Znth_app_l : forall n (a: A) l1 l2,
  (0 <= n) -> 
  (n < Zlength l1) ->
  replace_Znth n (l1 ++ l2) a = replace_Znth n l1 a ++ l2.
Proof.
  intros.
  unfold replace_Znth.
  apply replace_nth_app_l.
  unfold Zlength in H0.
  lia.
Qed. 

Lemma replace_Znth_app_r : forall n (a: A) l1 l2,
  (n >= Zlength l1) ->
  replace_Znth n (l1 ++ l2) a = replace_Znth n l1 a ++ replace_Znth (n - Zlength l1) l2 a.
Proof. 
  intros.
  unfold replace_Znth.
  unfold Zlength in *.
  replace (Z.to_nat (n - Z.of_nat (length l1))) with (Z.to_nat n - length l1)%nat by lia.
  rewrite replace_nth_app_r ; try lia.
  auto.
Qed.


Lemma replace_Znth_nothing : forall n (l: list A) (a: A),
  n >= Zlength l -> replace_Znth n l a = l.
Proof.
  intros.
  unfold Zlength in H.
  unfold replace_Znth.
  assert (Z.to_nat n >= length l)%nat by lia.
  clear H. 
  set (m := Z.to_nat n) in *; clearbody m ; clear n.
  generalize dependent l.
  induction m; intros. 
  + destruct l; simpl in * ; auto ; lia.
  + destruct l; simpl in * ; auto.
    rewrite IHm ; auto. lia. 
Qed.

Qed.

(* replace_Znth *)

Lemma replace_Znth_cons: forall n (a b: A) l,
  n > 0 ->
  replace_Znth n (b :: l) a =
  b :: replace_Znth (n - 1) l a.
Proof.
  intros.
  unfold replace_Znth.
  assert (Z.to_nat n = S (Z.to_nat (n - 1))) by lia.
  rewrite H0.
  simpl.
  reflexivity.
Qed.


Lemma replace_Znth_Znth: forall n l (a0: A),

Lemma proof_of_array_add_safety_wit_2 : array_add_safety_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  entailer!.
  pose proof (H13 i).
  assert (0 <= i < n_pre) by lia.
  specialize (H14 H15).
  lia.
  pose proof (H13 i).

  specialize (H14 H15).
  lia.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_array_add_entail_wit_1 : array_add_entail_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  Exists lo.
  Exists (@nil Z).
  entailer!.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_array_add_entail_wit_2 : array_add_entail_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  assert (Zlength (sublist (i_2 + 1) n_pre lo) = n_pre - (i_2 + 1)).
  {
    rewrite Zlength_sublist; lia.
  }
  assert (
    forall k_2 : Z,
      0 <= k_2 < n_pre - (i_2 + 1) ->
      Znth k_2 (sublist (i_2 + 1) n_pre lo) 0 = Znth (i_2 + 1 + k_2) lo 0).
  {
    intros k_2 Hrange.
    rewrite (Znth_sublist (i_2 + 1) k_2 n_pre lo 0) by lia.
    replace (k_2 + (i_2 + 1)) with (i_2 + 1 + k_2) by lia.
    reflexivity.
  }
  Exists (sublist (i_2 + 1) n_pre lo).
  Exists l1'.
  entailer!.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_array_add_return_wit_1 : array_add_return_wit_1.
Proof.

  2: lia.
  sep_apply (IntArray.full_split_to_missing_i b_pre i n_pre lb 0).
  2: lia.
  sep_apply (IntArray.full_split_to_missing_i out_pre i n_pre (l1 ++ l2) 0).
  2: lia.

  rewrite H4.

  pose proof (H7 0) as Hsuffix0.
  assert (0 <= 0 < n_pre - i) as Hzero by lia.
  specialize (Hsuffix0 Hzero).
  replace (i + 0) with i in Hsuffix0 by lia.

  exact Hsuffix0.

  rewrite (sublist_split i n_pre (i + 1) lo) by (pose proof (Zlength_correct lo); lia).
  rewrite (sublist_single i lo 0) by (pose proof (Zlength_correct lo); lia).

  assert (

From AUXLib Require Import int_auto Axioms Feq Idents List_lemma VMap.

From SimpleC.EE.CAV.verify_20260418_124259_string_copy Require Import string_copy_goal.

Lemma proof_of_string_copy_entail_wit_1 : string_copy_entail_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  Exists d nil.
  rewrite (logic_equiv_sepcon_comm
             (CharArray.full src_pre (n + 1) (l ++ 0 :: nil))
             (CharArray.full dst_pre (n + 1) d)).
  prop_apply CharArray.full_length. Intros.
  rewrite (logic_equiv_sepcon_comm
             (CharArray.full dst_pre (n + 1) d)
             (CharArray.full src_pre (n + 1) (l ++ 0 :: nil))).
  entailer!.
  - rewrite Zlength_correct.
    lia.
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_string_copy_entail_wit_2 : string_copy_entail_wit_2.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  rewrite (logic_equiv_sepcon_comm
             (CharArray.full dst_pre (n + 1)
                (replace_Znth i (Znth i (l ++ 0 :: nil) 0) (l1_2 ++ d1_2)))
             (CharArray.full src_pre (n + 1) (l ++ 0 :: nil))).
  prop_apply CharArray.full_length. Intros.
  rewrite (logic_equiv_sepcon_comm
             (CharArray.full src_pre (n + 1) (l ++ 0 :: nil))
             (CharArray.full dst_pre (n + 1)
                (replace_Znth i (Znth i (l ++ 0 :: nil) 0) (l1_2 ++ d1_2)))).
  assert (Hlen_l : Zlength l = n).
  {
    match goal with
    | Hlen : Z.of_nat (Datatypes.length (l ++ 0 :: nil)) = n + 1 |- _ =>
        rewrite <- Zlength_correct in Hlen;
        rewrite Zlength_app_cons in Hlen;
        lia
    end.
  }
  assert (Hlen_nat : Z.of_nat (Datatypes.length l) = n).
  {
    rewrite <- Zlength_correct.
    exact Hlen_l.
  }
  assert (Hi_lt_n : i < n).
  {
    destruct (Z_lt_ge_dec i n) as [Hlt | Hge].
    - exact Hlt.
    - assert (i = n) by lia.
      subst i.
      assert (Hlen_l1_2 : Zlength l1_2 = n) by lia.
      rewrite Hlen_l1_2 in H.
      rewrite app_Znth2 in H by lia.
      replace (n - Zlength l) with 0 in H by lia.
      cbn in H.
      exfalso.
      apply H.
      reflexivity.
  }
  destruct d1_2 eqn:Hd1.
  - rewrite Zlength_nil in H4. lia.
  - simpl in H4.
  assert (Hdst :
    replace_Znth i (Znth i (l ++ 0 :: nil) 0) (l1_2 ++ z :: l0) =
    (l1_2 ++ cons (Znth i l 0) nil) ++ l0).
  {
    rewrite app_Znth1 by lia.
    rewrite replace_Znth_app_r by lia.
    rewrite replace_Znth_nothing by lia.
    replace (i - Zlength l1_2) with 0 by lia.
    simpl.
    rewrite <- app_assoc.
    reflexivity.
  }
  Exists l0 (l1_2 ++ cons (Znth i l 0) nil).
  rewrite Hdst.
  entailer!;
  [ intros k Hk;
    destruct (Z_lt_ge_dec k i) as [Hlt | Hge];
    [ apply H10; lia
    | assert (k = i) by lia;
      subst k;
      rewrite app_Znth1 in H by lia;
      exact H ]
  | intros k Hk;
    destruct (Z_lt_ge_dec k i) as [Hlt | Hge];
    [ rewrite app_Znth1 by lia;
      apply H5;
      lia
    | assert (k = i) by lia;
      subst k;
      rewrite app_Znth2 by lia;
      replace (i - Zlength l1_2) with 0 by lia;
      rewrite Znth0_cons;
      reflexivity ]
  | rewrite Zlength_cons in H4; lia
  | rewrite Zlength_app, Zlength_cons, Zlength_nil; lia ].
Qed.

Lemma proof_of_string_copy_return_wit_1 : string_copy_return_wit_1.
Proof.
  pre_process.
  rewrite (logic_equiv_sepcon_comm
             (CharArray.full dst_pre (n + 1) (replace_Znth i 0 (l1 ++ d1)))
             (CharArray.full src_pre (n + 1) (l ++ 0 :: nil))).
  prop_apply CharArray.full_length. Intros.
  rewrite (logic_equiv_sepcon_comm
             (CharArray.full src_pre (n + 1) (l ++ 0 :: nil))
             (CharArray.full dst_pre (n + 1) (replace_Znth i 0 (l1 ++ d1)))).
  assert (Hlen_l : Zlength l = n).
  {
    match goal with
    | Hlen : Z.of_nat (Datatypes.length (l ++ 0 :: nil)) = n + 1 |- _ =>
        rewrite <- Zlength_correct in Hlen;
        rewrite Zlength_app_cons in Hlen;
        lia
    end.
  }
  assert (Hlen_nat : Z.of_nat (Datatypes.length l) = n).
  {
    rewrite <- Zlength_correct.
    exact Hlen_l.
  }
  assert (Hi_eq_n : i = n).
  {
    destruct (Z_lt_ge_dec i n) as [Hlt | Hge].
    - specialize (H10 i).
      assert (Znth i l 0 <> 0) by (apply H10; lia).
      rewrite app_Znth1 in H by lia.
      contradiction.
    - lia.
  }
  subst i.
  assert (Hl1_eq_l : l1 = l).
  {
    apply (proj2 (list_eq_ext 0 l1 l)).
    split.
    - lia.
    - intros k Hk.
      apply H5.
      lia.
  }
  subst l1.
  assert (Hd1_single : exists x, d1 = x :: nil).
  {
    destruct d1.
    - rewrite Zlength_nil in H4. lia.
    - rewrite Zlength_cons in H4.
      destruct d1.
      + eexists. reflexivity.
      + repeat rewrite Zlength_cons in H4.
        pose proof Zlength_nonneg d1.
        lia.
  }
  destruct Hd1_single.
  subst d1.
  rewrite replace_Znth_app_r by lia.
  rewrite replace_Znth_nothing by lia.
  replace (n - Zlength l) with 0 by lia.
  unfold replace_Znth.
  replace (Z.to_nat (Zlength l - Zlength l)) with 0%nat by lia.
  simpl.
  entailer!.
Qed.

Require Import ZArith.
Require Import Coq.Lists.List.

Open Scope Z_scope.

Fixpoint max_list_nonempty (l : list Z) : Z :=
  match l with
  | nil => 0
  | x :: nil => x
  | x :: xs => Z.max x (max_list_nonempty xs)
  end.

item.text: The compile failure is load-path, not proof logic: `Require Import array_max` in the generated files needs the repository’s `input/array_max.v` on Coq’s search path. I’m recording that and rerunning the compile chain with an extra `-Q` for `CAV/input`, which is the least invasive fix for this workspace.

item.text: The proof state shows this is not a Coq trick problem. `safety_wit_5` is missing an explicit bound strong enough to prove `i + 1 <= INT_MAX`, which means the invariant is still too weak for the generated VC. I’m checking the nearby repo examples for the standard fix, then I’ll push that bound back into the annotated loop state and rerun `symexec` as required.

item.aggregated_output:
## Loop reasoning for `is_sorted_nondecreasing`

### Round 1: identify the loop summary

The function scans adjacent pairs from left to right and returns `0` immediately when it finds a descent. If the loop finishes, the postcondition for return value `1` needs a universal statement that every adjacent pair in the whole array is nondecreasing.

The natural summary at loop head is therefore:

- `i` is the next adjacent pair index to check.
- Every pair strictly before `i` has already been checked and satisfies `l[j] <= l[j + 1]`.
- The array contents and scalar parameter `n` are unchanged, so keep `a == a@pre` and `n == n@pre`.
- Preserve `IntArray::full(a, n, l)` through the whole loop.

This yields the candidate invariant:

- `0 <= i <= n`
- `(forall (j: Z), (0 <= j && j + 1 < i) => l[j] <= l[j + 1])`
- unchanged-parameter equalities
- full array ownership

Initialization is immediate at `i = 0` because the quantified premise `j + 1 < 0` is impossible.

Preservation also matches the control flow:

- if `a[i] <= a[i + 1]`, then after `++i` the processed prefix grows by one pair, so the quantified range extends from `< i` to `< i + 1`;
- if `a[i] > a[i + 1]`, the function returns immediately, so this branch should expose the witness pair `i`.

### Round 2: place the minimum assertions

Two assertions are needed at control-point boundaries.

1. Early-return assertion inside the `if` branch.

This assertion fixes:

- `0 <= i && i + 1 < n`, coming from the loop guard;
- `l[i] > l[i + 1]`, matching the existential witness required by the `__return == 0` postcondition;
- unchanged parameters and array ownership.

This should let the generated `return_wit` choose witness `i` directly.

2. Loop-exit assertion right after the `for`.

At loop exit we know `!(i + 1 < n)`, so record `i + 1 >= n`. Combined with the invariant and `0 <= i`, this is enough to strengthen the prefix property from checked pairs `< i` to all pairs `< n`: any `j` with `j + 1 < n` also satisfies `j + 1 <= i`, hence `j + 1 < i` or equivalently `j < i`.

I therefore asserted the final universal property directly after the loop, together with `i + 1 >= n`, unchanged parameters, and full ownership. This keeps the assertion close to the loop exit and avoids pushing exit reasoning down to the final return statement.

### Round 3: fix the missing integer-range fact exposed by proof debugging

The first manual proof attempt revealed that `safety_wit_2` still asked for a proof of `i + 1` being in signed-int range at loop head. Preserving only `0 <= i <= n` was not enough: from `i` being an `int` we get `i <= INT_MAX`, but that still allows `i = INT_MAX`, which does not imply `i + 1 <= INT_MAX`.

This is therefore an annotation-layer gap rather than a Coq-layer gap. The invariant has been strengthened with:

- `i + 1 <= INT_MAX`

and the loop-exit assertion now keeps the same bound. This is the minimum extra fact needed for the tool to justify the loop-condition arithmetic on `i + 1` without redesigning the contract or changing the functional reasoning.

### Round 4: preserve the bound on `n` explicitly

After regenerating the witnesses, the continue-branch proof obligation still needed to establish the next-iteration bound `i + 2 <= INT_MAX`. The branch assumptions only expose:

- `i + 1 < n`
- the current invariant facts

so the proof can only derive `i + 2 <= INT_MAX` if the invariant also carries an explicit upper bound on `n`.

I therefore added:

- `n <= INT_MAX`

to the invariant and the loop-exit assertion. Together with `i + 1 < n`, this gives the next-head range fact by pure arithmetic, while remaining consistent with the C type of `n` and the fixed contract.

## Verification issues for `is_sorted_nondecreasing`

- Stage: `annotation`
  - Symptom: the first generated manual proof contained `proof_of_is_sorted_nondecreasing_safety_wit_2`, asking for `i + 1` to stay within signed-int range at loop head.
  - Trigger: the original loop invariant preserved `0 <= i <= n` and prefix sortedness, but did not preserve an explicit range fact for the expression `i + 1`.
  - Diagnosis: inspecting `is_sorted_nondecreasing_goal.v` showed `safety_wit_2` required `((i + 1) <= INT_MAX)`.
  - Fix: strengthened the invariant and loop-exit assertion with `i + 1 <= INT_MAX`, then cleared generated Coq files and reran `symexec`.
  - Result: `safety_wit_2` disappeared from `proof_manual.v`.

- Stage: `annotation`
  - Symptom: after the first annotation fix, the regenerated proof obligations still contained a nontrivial continue-branch entailment.
  - Trigger: `entail_wit_3` needed to prove the next-head bound `((i + 1) + 1) <= INT_MAX`.
  - Diagnosis: the branch assumptions exposed `i + 1 < n_pre`, but the invariant did not carry any explicit upper bound on `n_pre`, so the next-head range fact could not be recovered in pure Coq.
  - Fix attempt: strengthened the invariant and loop-exit assertion with `n <= INT_MAX` and reran `symexec`.
  - Result: `entail_wit_3` disappeared, but a new initialization obligation appeared in `entail_wit_1`.

- Stage: `proof / contract boundary`
  - Symptom: the current `proof_manual.v` still cannot be completed.
  - Trigger: the regenerated `entail_wit_1` now requires proving `n_pre <= INT_MAX` from the function precondition alone.
  - Diagnosis: the current input contract does not state any upper bound on `n`, and the generated initialization witness does not retain the local `n` store, so Verify has no authorized way to derive that bound. This makes the strengthened invariant unprovable from the formal input.
  - Fix action considered: changing `input/is_sorted_nondecreasing.c` to add `n <= INT_MAX` (or a tighter bound) would likely unblock the proof, but that is a Contract-stage modification and is outside Verify permissions.
  - Result: verification remains blocked at the formal-input boundary. `goal_check.v` has not been compiled successfully end to end.

- Files produced successfully before the blocker:
  - `logs/qcp_run.log`

  - `coq/generated/is_sorted_nondecreasing_proof_auto.v`
  - `coq/generated/is_sorted_nondecreasing_proof_manual.v`
  - `coq/generated/is_sorted_nondecreasing_goal_check.v`

- Compile status:

  - `proof_manual.v` failed with an incomplete proof at `proof_of_is_sorted_nondecreasing_entail_wit_1`
  - `goal_check.v` was not compiled successfully after the manual-proof failure

item.text: The rerun did what we wanted: the problematic safety and `> cur` branch witnesses disappeared. The regenerated proof surface is smaller and cleaner now, so I’m rewriting `proof_manual.v` against this new witness set instead of trying to salvage the old scripts.

item.text: The current blocker is now well-characterized: the strengthened invariant removes the loop-range witnesses, but it introduces `n_pre <= INT_MAX` into `entail_wit_1`, and that fact is not derivable from the current formal input. I’m collecting the final timestamps and artifact state, then I’ll write the required `issues.md` and `metrics.md` as a contract-boundary failure.

I recorded the full chain in [issues.md](/home/yangfp/QualifiedCProgramming/QCP_examples/CAV/output/verify_20260418_205317_array_prefix_max/logs/issues.md), [annotation_reasoning.md](/home/yangfp/QualifiedCProgramming/QCP_examples/CAV/output/verify_20260418_205317_array_prefix_max/logs/annotation_reasoning.md), [proof_reasoning.md](/home/yangfp/QualifiedCProgramming/QCP_examples/CAV/output/verify_20260418_205317_array_prefix_max/logs/proof_reasoning.md), and [metrics.md](/home/yangfp/QualifiedCProgramming/QCP_examples/CAV/output/verify_20260418_205317_array_prefix_max/logs/metrics.md). `Final Result: Fail` is written there.
